qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 学习dev系列commands对close -> commit/merge -> push 工作流的适配，将这个结尾工作流也加入到clean的适配。同时告诉我还有哪些command 也需要加收尾工作流的适配

complaint_element_pointer.selector: commands/clean.md line 589 (Step 17 Next Steps block, 'If satisfied' branch)

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: commands/clean.md line 589 now reads: 'If satisfied: Changes already committed — next step is /push to publish to remote (push-gate note: ...)'. The /push direction is present. Both branches (satisfied → /push; unsatisfied → git reset) are present at lines 589-590.
qa.overall_assessment: AC3 passes (cleaner.md unchanged, /close not imposed on clean). AC2 passes on the actionable dimension (Tier A/B enumeration of file-modifying commands is complete; two missing commands are read-only and correctly excluded from Tiers A/B). AC1 fails: the Step 17 next-step wording includes 'run bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly' as the primary workaround — an inline bash invocation that violates BA spec AC1's explicit constraint 'direction toward next command, not inline git invocations.' The push-gate architectural gap (cleaner does not write push-gate token so bare /push would exit 3) means the inline bash workaround is technically necessary, but the BA spec requires a different resolution: either fix the cleaner to write the push-gate token (out of scope for this cycle) or document the limitation without providing the inline invocation.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: commands/doc-gen.md is absent from the enumeration in both BA spec and dev report. It is a read-only analysis command (allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob only) and would correctly be Tier C. The BA spec's claimed count of 33 commands is therefore off by 2 (actual: 35 commands excluding INDEX.md and README.md). Actionable Tier A/B coverage is unaffected.

commands/security-check.md is absent from the enumeration in both BA spec and dev report. It is a read-only analysis command (allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash for analysis) and would correctly be Tier C. Does not affect Tier A/B completeness.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: 将这个结尾工作流也加入到clean的适配
success_criteria_results.criterion: clean.md Step 17/18 gains a next-step block directing user to /push when satisfied and checkpoint rollback when not; /close gate is NOT imposed
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read commands/clean.md lines 580-595; grep for /close in entire file; grep for /push in Step 17 block
success_criteria_results.details: BRANCH COVERAGE: Both satisfied and unsatisfied branches are present (lines 589 and 590). /close does NOT appear anywhere in clean.md (grep confirmed zero matches). /push IS present in line 589. HOWEVER: BA spec AC1 requires 'direction toward next command, not inline git invocations.' Line 589 offers 'run bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly' as the primary workaround — this is an inline bash invocation. The secondary option '/commit then /push' IS proper command-chain guidance. The push-gate architectural constraint makes bare /push non-functional without either the inline bash or the /commit detour, but the BA spec forbids inline bash as the documented resolution.
success_criteria_results.evidence: commands/clean.md:589 — 'If satisfied: Changes already committed — next step is /push to publish to remote (push-gate note: the cleaner commits inline without writing a push-gate token; run bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly, or run /commit then /push if you need the full gate chain)'. BA spec AC1: 'consistent with how dev.md Step 15 phrases its chain pointer (direction toward next command, not inline git invocation)'. hooks/push.sh:84-125 confirms push-gate token required at /tmp/agentic-commit/push/<repo_hash>/<branch>.json. agents/cleaner.md: no push-gate token writing found anywhere.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: 同时告诉我还有哪些command 也需要加收尾工作流的适配
success_criteria_results.criterion: Concrete enumeration with per-command tier (A/B/C) and justification covering all commands/*.md files
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Glob commands/*.md to get actual file list; compare against enumeration in dev report and BA spec; check Tier A/B completeness for file-modifying commands
success_criteria_results.details: Tier A: 1 command (dev-command.md) — correctly identified as HIGH priority gap. Tier B: 4 commands (clean.md, doc-sync.md, quick-commit.md, quick-prototype.md) — correctly identified with justified gaps and priorities. Tier C: 29 commands enumerated with concrete justifications. Two commands (doc-gen.md, security-check.md) are absent from the enumeration but both are read-only analysis tools that would be Tier C; their absence does not affect the actionable finding (no file-modifying command is missing from Tiers A/B). BA spec AC2 literal wording: 'no command in commands/*.md that modifies files and lacks /push guidance is missing from Tier A or Tier B' — this requirement IS satisfied. Count discrepancy (claimed 33, actual 35 commands) is a minor accuracy issue noted as out-of-scope observation.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Glob output: 37 .md files = 35 commands + INDEX.md + README.md. dev-report-20260517-172600.json command_enumeration_finding section contains full Tier A/B/C tables. Tier A: [dev-command.md]. Tier B: [clean.md, doc-sync.md, quick-commit.md, quick-prototype.md]. Tier C: 29 commands. All file-modifying commands lacking /push are in Tiers A/B.

qa._success_criteria_rule: BA output has 2 items in requirements_decomposition (R1, R2); QA produced 2 entries.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The architectural root cause (cleaner does not write push-gate token → bare /push exits 3 after /clean) is documented but not resolved. The workaround text in clean.md line 589 acknowledges the gap. Dev report explicitly sets scope_review_requested=true and recommends a follow-up cycle for the cleaner to write the push-gate token. The documentation change partially addresses the user-visible symptom (users now have guidance) but the primary workaround (inline bash invocation) violates the BA spec wording constraint.

build_verification.skip_reason: doc-only change to commands/clean.md (.md file); no compile step, no deployed service, no UI rendering. Context JSON setup.applicability = N/A confirmed.

regression_test_results.details: Line 468 (checkpoint creation guard) and line 479 (cleaner subagent delegation mandate) are present and unchanged per grep output. The single line edit at 589 does not touch these guards.

Read agents/cleaner.md in full (148 lines). No modifications present. Git operations section (lines 94-102) shows inline commit behavior is unchanged: 'Stage changes after each action category, then create a single commit'. No push-gate token writing added.
Grep for '/close' in commands/clean.md returned zero matches. The /close command does not appear anywhere in the file.

code_quality_findings.issue: BA spec and context JSON claim 'All 33 commands/*.md files are classified' but filesystem has 35 actual commands. Count is off by 2 (missing: doc-gen.md, security-check.md). Both missing commands are Tier C read-only tools; actionable coverage is complete.
code_quality_findings.recommendation: Correct count to 35 in next iteration or note the two missing files explicitly as Tier C in the enumeration for complete accuracy.

e2e_enforcement.health_check_result: N/A — doc-only change; no web service involved

ui_test_results.reason: Change is documentation only (.md file). No UI, no browser, no service. Context JSON setup.applicability=N/A confirmed.

all_findings.issue: AC1 violation: BA spec requires 'direction toward next command, not inline git invocations' but line 589 includes 'run bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly' as the primary workaround. This is an inline bash invocation. The push-gate architectural gap (cleaner does not write the push-gate token at /tmp/agentic-commit/push/<repo_hash>/<branch>.json) makes bare /push non-functional, but the correct resolution per BA spec is either (a) fix the cleaner to write the push-gate token (follow-up cycle) or (b) document the limitation without providing inline bash as guidance. The secondary option '/commit then /push' in the same line IS compliant command-chain guidance.
all_findings.recommendation: Remove 'run bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly' from line 589. Change to: 'If satisfied: Changes already committed — run /commit then /push to publish to remote (note: the cleaner's inline commit does not write a push-gate token; /commit will create one, enabling /push)'. File a follow-up cycle to have agents/cleaner.md write the push-gate token after its inline commit, which would allow bare /push to work.

all_findings.issue: Enumeration count claim is off: stated '33 commands' but actual filesystem has 35 commands (excluding INDEX.md and README.md). Two commands absent from all tiers: commands/doc-gen.md (read-only doc generation) and commands/security-check.md (read-only security analysis). Both would correctly be Tier C. Tier A/B completeness (file-modifying commands) is unaffected — this is a count accuracy issue only.
all_findings.recommendation: Add doc-gen.md and security-check.md to Tier C in the enumeration with reason 'Read-only analysis; produces documentation/reports only, modifies no files'. Correct count claim to 35.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec blocked by pretool-tool-policy.py hook (QA agent write restriction prevents codex from writing output to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/). Same hook pattern as BA's codex attempt in session context. Self-review substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review covered 5 adversarial questions: (1) Is FAIL on AC1 too strict given push-gate reality? — FAIL maintained; inline bash is clearly stated as prohibited by BA spec; the /commit→/push path is available and should be primary. (2) Is FAIL on AC2 appropriate for 2 missing Tier C commands? — Downgraded to minor/pass because AC2's literal wording gates on file-modifying commands missing from Tiers A/B; both absent commands are read-only. (3) Regression risk from single-line edit? — Low; guards at lines 468/479 confirmed intact. (4) Both branches present per AC1? — Yes; lines 589 and 590 cover satisfied→/push and unsatisfied→rollback. (5) Does /commit→/push alternative satisfy AC1 spirit? — Yes, but the primary offered path is the inline bash invocation, which violates the AC1 constraint.

failed_criteria: R1 / AC1: clean.md Step 17 adaptation violates BA spec AC1 wording constraint — 'direction toward next command, not inline git invocations'; current line 589 leads with 'bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly'
major_issues: commands/clean.md:589 — Remove inline bash invocation 'bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly'. Replace with /commit→/push command-chain guidance as the primary path. Add parenthetical note: '(note: the cleaner's inline commit does not write a push-gate token; /commit will create one)'. The push-gate token writing by agents/cleaner.md should be addressed in a follow-up cycle.
refined_context.recommended_approach: Dev must update line 589 of commands/clean.md to remove the 'run bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly' workaround text. The correct guidance is '/commit then /push' as the primary path. The parenthetical note about push-gate token can remain to explain WHY /commit is needed first, but the inline bash invocation must be removed. Separately, a follow-up cycle should modify agents/cleaner.md to write the push-gate token after its inline commit (same format as changelog-analyst Phase 10), which would eventually enable bare /push after /clean.
refined_context.additional_context: hooks/push.sh lines 84-125 confirmed: push-gate token path is /tmp/agentic-commit/push/<sha256-of-repo-root-16chars>/<branch-with-slashes-as-doublunderscores>.json. The cleaner's inline git commit does not create this file. agents/cleaner.md (full read, 148 lines) confirms no push-gate writing. The /commit command (commands/commit.md) is the correct mechanism to create the push-gate token.
